chunk_summary: Iron deficiency treatment is integral to managing feeding difficulties and faltering growth in India, with national anemia programs providing structured supplementation and deworming schedules.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of iron deficiency in feeding difficulties and faltering growth.
Learn the therapeutic guidelines for treating pediatric iron deficiency anemia in India.
Explore the linkage between anemia treatment and national supplementation programs like AMB and I-NIPI.
Recognize when to escalate evaluation for non-responsive or severe anemia cases.
key_facts: Iron deficiency contributes to fatigue, irritability, and poor appetite, exacerbated by restricted diets and excessive milk intake.
Therapeutic dose for iron deficiency anemia: 3 mg/kg/day elemental iron, with hemoglobin response expected within 2–4 weeks.
National AMB program provides biweekly IFA syrup (20 mg elemental iron + 100 mcg folic acid) for ages 6–59 months.
Deworming is paired with anemia treatment: albendazole 200 mg for ages 1–2 years, 400 mg for ages 2–19 years.
Post-correction iron supplementation continues for months to replete stores, followed by prophylaxis as per program schedules.
reference_quote: Indian pediatric anemia guidance supports a therapeutic trial of oral iron when iron deficiency is likely, with response assessed by a meaningful hemoglobin rise (e.g., >1 g/dL within 4 weeks in mild anemia).
document_key_topics: Escalation protocols for severe or recurrent anemia
